macro_events.event_summary: VIX spiked 9.7% as rising oil prices drove broader market fear and volatility.
primary_drivers: Higher crude oil prices flowing through to input costs and inflation expectations
Volatility spike increasing demand for hedges and volatility-linked products
Risk-off rotation away from consumer discretionary and into energy/defensives

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with direct upstream leverage to crude prices; each $10/bbl move meaningfully shifts upstream earnings. Pure-play beneficiary of the oil spike driving the fear.
Same direct upstream crude price leverage as XOM; US large-cap integrated that typically leads oil-driven sector rotations.
Permian-weighted E&P with high oil price beta (roughly 2x crude moves historically); pure-play US shale producer benefits directly from oil fear rally.
Pure-play E&P with no downstream offset; leveraged directly to WTI/Brent spot prices driving today's move.
Operator of the VIX complex and SPX options — volatility spikes drive options volumes and VIX product trading, a direct revenue tailwind.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs for Southwest and it has historically been less hedged than peers; direct margin hit from oil spike.
Jet fuel cost pass-through with lag; oil spikes compress near-term airline margins and airlines are the classic funding short in oil-fear trades.
Cruise operator with heavy bunker fuel exposure plus discretionary travel demand sensitivity — hit on both cost and demand sides from oil-driven risk-off.
Consumer discretionary plus freight/trucking oil cost exposure — wait, replacing with more direct name.
Last-mile logistics and freight fuel costs plus discretionary consumer exposure; typical underperformer in oil-shock risk-off tapes.

headlines: CBOE VIX Index Spikes 9.7% as Oil-Fueled Fears Grip Markets

macro_events.event_summary: Crypto ETFs recorded their largest weekly inflow since January, signaling renewed institutional demand for digital asset exposure.
primary_drivers: Institutional flows into spot crypto ETFs increase AUM-based fees for issuers
Rising BTC/ETH spot demand from ETF creations lifts crypto-correlated equities
Positive flow momentum re-rates miners and exchanges via higher transaction/fee volume expectations

beneficiaries.rationale: Coinbase is custodian for most major spot BTC ETFs (including IBIT) and earns custody fees plus benefits from rising retail trading volumes that typically accompany ETF inflow cycles. Direct revenue link to crypto AUM and volume.
MicroStrategy holds ~500K+ BTC on balance sheet and trades as a leveraged BTC proxy with a persistent NAV premium that expands during inflow-driven rallies.
Largest publicly traded Bitcoin miner; revenue is direct function of BTC price, and miners show high beta (1.5-2x) to BTC moves during inflow periods.
Pure-play BTC miner with significant treasury holdings; historically rallies alongside ETF inflow cycles via BTC price beta.
Robinhood derives a meaningful and growing share of transaction revenue from crypto; renewed retail interest following ETF inflows typically lifts crypto trading volumes on the platform.

headlines: Crypto ETFs See Biggest Weekly Inflow Since January
